applicationInfo.name: ABCA - Autonomous Background Coding Agents on AWS
applicationInfo.description:
### Summary
Self-hosted platform for autonomous coding agents that work in isolated cloud environments, cloning repos, writing code, running tests, and creating pull requests without human interaction during execution.

### Key objectives
- Provide autonomous background coding agents running on AWS infrastructure
- Execute coding tasks in isolated, ephemeral environments with no human interaction
- Generate pull requests for code review and integration
- Learn and improve from past task interactions through tiered memory system
- Maintain security through VPC isolation and least-privilege access controls

### Use-cases
- Submit coding tasks via CLI, webhooks, or REST API for autonomous execution
- Repository onboarding with per-repo configuration and GitHub App integration
- Background code generation, bug fixes, and feature implementation
- Automated testing and validation of generated code changes
- Pull request creation with artifacts and proof of functionality

### Features
- AgentCore Runtime with MicroVM isolation for secure agent execution
- Durable orchestrator using Lambda functions for task lifecycle management
- Multi-modal input support (text, images) for task descriptions
- Tiered memory system with AgentCore Memory for cross-task learning
- REST API with Cognito authentication and webhook integration
- CDK-based infrastructure with modular construct architecture
- Cost optimization through configurable compute strategies and model selection

architecture.description:
### High Level Architecture

#### Application type
Cloud-native autonomous coding agent platform - serverless web application with CLI and API interfaces

#### Layers
- Presentation: CLI client, REST API, webhooks, web dashboard
- Orchestration: Lambda-based task orchestrator with durable execution
- Compute: AgentCore Runtime with MicroVM isolation for agent execution
- Data: DynamoDB tables for state, AgentCore Memory for cross-task learning
- Security: Cognito authentication, VPC isolation, Bedrock Guardrails

#### Boundaries
- External: GitHub repositories, Bedrock AI models, user CLI/API clients
- Internal: VPC-isolated agent runtime, DynamoDB state persistence
- Trust: Cognito user pools, HMAC webhook authentication, IAM roles

#### Technology components
- AWS CDK with TypeScript for infrastructure as code
- Python agent runtime with Claude Agent SDK
- Node.js Lambda functions for API handlers and orchestration
- AWS AgentCore Runtime for secure agent execution
- Amazon Bedrock for AI model access (Claude Sonnet/Haiku)
- DynamoDB for task state and event persistence
- Amazon Cognito for user authentication

#### Deployment architecture
- Single AWS region deployment via CDK stack
- VPC with private subnets and NAT gateway for agent isolation
- API Gateway with WAF protection and Cognito authorization
- Lambda functions with ARM64 architecture

#### APIs

| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |
|----------|--------|----------|
| /tasks | POST | Create new coding task |
| /tasks | GET | List user tasks |
| /tasks/{id} | GET | Get task details |
| /tasks/{id} | DELETE | Cancel running task |
| /tasks/{id}/events | GET | Get task event history |
| /webhooks | POST | Create webhook integration |
| /webhooks/{id} | DELETE | Revoke webhook |

#### Security relevant components
- VPC isolation with DNS firewall for agent network restrictions
- Bedrock Guardrails for prompt injection detection
- Secrets Manager for GitHub tokens and webhook secrets
- IAM least-privilege roles with resource-level permissions
- WAF with managed rule sets and rate limiting

### Low Level Architecture
- AgentStack CDK construct orchestrating all AWS resources
- TaskOrchestrator Lambda with durable execution for task lifecycle
- TaskApi construct with Cognito authentication and Lambda integrations
- AgentVpc construct providing network isolation for agent runtime
- Blueprint construct for repository onboarding configuration
- TaskTable, TaskEventsTable for state persistence with TTL
- AgentMemory construct for cross-task learning and knowledge retention
- DnsFirewall for domain-level egress filtering
- ConcurrencyReconciler for user task limit enforcement
- TaskDashboard for operational monitoring and observability

dataflow.description:
### Human actors
- Developers using CLI to submit coding tasks
- Operators monitoring system via dashboard
- Repository maintainers receiving pull requests
- GitHub webhook callers triggering tasks

### External entities
- GitHub API for repository operations and issue data
- Amazon Bedrock for AI model inference (Claude Sonnet/Haiku)
- GitHub repositories containing source code to modify
- External webhook callers (CI/CD systems, issue trackers)

### Processes
- TaskApi Lambda handlers processing REST requests
- TaskOrchestrator managing task lifecycle with durable execution
- AgentCore Runtime executing isolated agent sessions
- ConcurrencyReconciler maintaining user task limits
- WebhookAuthorizer validating HMAC signatures

### Data stores

| Store | Type | High-Value (Y/N) |
|-------|------|------------------|
| TaskTable | DynamoDB | Y |
| TaskEventsTable | DynamoDB | Y |
| UserConcurrencyTable | DynamoDB | N |
| WebhookTable | DynamoDB | Y |
| RepoTable | DynamoDB | N |
| AgentMemory | Bedrock Memory | Y |
| Secrets Manager | AWS Service | Y |

### Trust boundaries

| Zone | Elements | Trust Level |
|------|----------|-------------|
| Public Internet | CLI clients, webhook callers | Untrusted |
| API Gateway | Cognito auth, WAF protection | Semi-trusted |
| VPC Private | Agent runtime, Lambda functions | Trusted |
| AWS Services | DynamoDB, Bedrock, Secrets Manager | Trusted |

### Data flows

| Flow ID | Description | Source | Target | Trust Boundaries Crossed | Assets |
|---------|-------------|--------|--------|--------------------------|--------|
| F1 | CLI task submission | Developer CLI | API Gateway | Public to Semi-trusted | Task specs, auth tokens |
| F2 | Webhook task creation | External webhook | API Gateway | Public to Semi-trusted | Task data, HMAC signature |
| F3 | Task orchestration | API Gateway | TaskOrchestrator | Semi-trusted to Trusted | Task records, user context |
| F4 | Agent execution | TaskOrchestrator | AgentCore Runtime | Trusted to Trusted | Code, prompts, GitHub tokens |
| F5 | GitHub operations | AgentCore Runtime | GitHub API | Trusted to Public | Repository data, pull requests |
| F6 | AI model inference | AgentCore Runtime | Bedrock | Trusted to Trusted | Prompts, code context |
| F7 | Memory operations | AgentCore Runtime | AgentMemory | Trusted to Trusted | Task episodes, learnings |
| F8 | State persistence | Lambda handlers | DynamoDB | Trusted to Trusted | Task state, events, metrics |
| F9 | Secret retrieval | AgentCore Runtime | Secrets Manager | Trusted to Trusted | GitHub tokens, webhook secrets |
| F10 | Dashboard queries | Operators | CloudWatch | Trusted to Trusted | Logs, metrics, traces |

assumptions.content: Code analysis based on current repository state represents complete and accurate system architecture

metadata.value: Analysis performed on static code repository without runtime verification of deployed infrastructure

assumptions.content: AWS CDK TypeScript application with Python agent runtime represents production-ready deployment model

metadata.value: Based on CDK constructs, TypeScript handlers, Python agent code, and AWS service integrations

assumptions.content: System operates in experimental/development status with iterative feature delivery as documented in roadmap

metadata.value: README indicates experimental status with active development across multiple iterations

assumptions.content: AgentCore Runtime and Bedrock services provide secure isolation and AI model access for agent execution

metadata.value: Based on AgentCore MicroVM isolation, VPC networking, and Bedrock model integration in stack definition

assumptions.content: Multi-component architecture with CLI, agent runtime, orchestrator, and API components work together cohesively

metadata.value: Assumption based on separate component directories and integration patterns in source code

assumptions.content: AgentCore Runtime provides secure MicroVM isolation sufficient for untrusted code execution

metadata.value: Based on AgentCore construct usage and VPC isolation patterns in stack definition

assumptions.content: Durable Lambda execution provides reliable task orchestration with checkpoint recovery

metadata.value: TaskOrchestrator uses durableConfig with 9-hour timeout and 14-day retention for long-running tasks

assumptions.content: GitHub API integration provides sufficient context for autonomous task execution

metadata.value: Agent fetches issues, creates branches, commits code, and generates pull requests via GitHub API

assumptions.content: Bedrock Claude models provide adequate reasoning capability for coding tasks

metadata.value: System uses Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Haiku 4.5 with cross-region inference profiles for availability

assumptions.content: DynamoDB provides sufficient performance and consistency for task state management

metadata.value: Multiple tables handle tasks, events, concurrency tracking, and webhook management with TTL

assumptions.content: CDK-based infrastructure deployment provides adequate operational control and repeatability

metadata.value: Single CDK stack with TypeScript constructs manages all AWS resources and configuration

assumptions.content: VPC network isolation with DNS firewall provides sufficient security boundary for agent execution

metadata.value: AgentVpc construct with private subnets, NAT gateway, and DNS firewall for domain filtering

assumptions.content: AgentCore Runtime provides sufficient isolation between concurrent agent sessions

metadata.value: Based on VPC isolation and MicroVM architecture described in CDK constructs

assumptions.content: GitHub API rate limits and authentication are handled appropriately by agent runtime

metadata.value: Agent performs multiple GitHub operations including cloning, branching, and PR creation

assumptions.content: DynamoDB provides adequate consistency for concurrent task state management

metadata.value: Multiple Lambda functions read/write task state with user concurrency limits enforced

assumptions.content: Webhook HMAC validation provides sufficient authentication for external task creation

metadata.value: Webhook authorizer validates signatures using secrets stored in Secrets Manager

assumptions.content: Bedrock Guardrails effectively detect and block prompt injection attacks in task descriptions

metadata.value: Task creation applies guardrails with HIGH strength for prompt attack detection

assumptions.content: Agent memory system provides value without leaking sensitive information across tasks

metadata.value: AgentMemory stores cross-task learnings and episodes for improved performance

assumptions.content: DNS firewall in observation mode provides adequate network egress monitoring

metadata.value: DNS firewall configured in observation mode for initial deployment with domain allowlists

assumptions.content: MicroVM isolation in AgentCore Runtime provides sufficient security boundary for untrusted code execution

metadata.value: Threat analysis assumes MicroVM technology provides adequate isolation between agent sessions and from underlying infrastructure. Any escape from this boundary would significantly impact system security.

assumptions.content: Bedrock Guardrails effectively detect and block sophisticated prompt injection attacks

metadata.value: Analysis assumes HIGH strength guardrails can prevent malicious prompt manipulation. Advanced prompt injection techniques may still bypass detection mechanisms.

assumptions.content: GitHub API rate limits and authentication mechanisms prevent abuse of repository operations

metadata.value: Assumes GitHub's built-in protections are sufficient for autonomous agent operations. High-frequency operations may trigger rate limiting or account suspension.

assumptions.content: WAF and API Gateway rate limiting provide adequate protection against volumetric attacks

metadata.value: Analysis assumes standard AWS WAF rules and rate limiting are sufficient. Sophisticated distributed attacks may still overwhelm these protections.

assumptions.content: DNS firewall in observation mode provides sufficient visibility without blocking legitimate traffic

metadata.value: Current observation mode allows all traffic while logging. Enforcement mode would be needed for active threat prevention but may impact legitimate operations.

mitigations.content: Implement multi-layered prompt injection detection using custom validation rules before Bedrock Guardrails, including pattern matching for common injection techniques, input sanitization, and task description length limits.

metadata.value: Combines pre-processing validation with Bedrock Guardrails for defense-in-depth against sophisticated prompt injection attacks. Includes rate limiting per user and task complexity analysis.

mitigations.content: Deploy behavioral monitoring for agent execution patterns to detect anomalous activities like excessive GitHub API calls, unusual repository access patterns, or attempts to access unauthorized resources.

metadata.value: CloudWatch-based monitoring with custom metrics and alarms for agent behavior patterns. Includes automated task termination for suspicious activities and alerting to security teams.

mitigations.content: Implement task sandboxing with read-only filesystem snapshots and network egress filtering to prevent malicious tasks from persisting changes or exfiltrating data outside approved channels.

metadata.value: Enhances MicroVM isolation with additional filesystem and network controls. Includes allowlist-based egress filtering and immutable base images for agent execution environments.

mitigations.content: Enable Cognito advanced security features including adaptive authentication, compromised credentials detection, and implement account lockout policies with progressive delays for failed login attempts.

metadata.value: Leverages Cognito's built-in security features to detect credential stuffing and brute force attacks. Includes integration with AWS WAF for additional IP-based blocking.

mitigations.content: Deploy distributed rate limiting using DynamoDB-backed counters with sliding window algorithms to track and limit webhook requests per source IP, HMAC signature, and time period.

metadata.value: Implements sophisticated rate limiting beyond basic WAF rules. Includes webhook source reputation scoring and automatic temporary blocking of abusive sources.

mitigations.content: Implement runtime attestation and integrity monitoring for AgentCore Runtime using AWS Nitro Enclaves or similar technology to detect MicroVM escape attempts and unauthorized access.

metadata.value: Provides continuous verification of agent runtime integrity and isolation boundaries. Includes automated incident response for detected compromise attempts.

mitigations.content: Deploy static code analysis and dependency scanning within the agent runtime before executing any repository code, with automatic quarantine of repositories containing suspicious patterns.

metadata.value: Integrates security scanning tools like CodeQL or Semgrep within the agent execution environment. Includes signature-based detection for known malicious patterns in dependencies.

mitigations.content: Implement infrastructure-as-code security controls with mandatory code review, automated security scanning of CDK templates, and deployment approval workflows with multi-person authorization.

metadata.value: Prevents unauthorized infrastructure changes through policy enforcement and review processes. Includes integration with AWS Config for continuous compliance monitoring.

mitigations.content: Deploy GitHub token rotation and scoping mechanisms with just-in-time access provisioning, limiting token permissions to specific repositories and operations required for each task.

metadata.value: Reduces blast radius of token compromise through dynamic scoping and automatic rotation. Includes audit logging of all token usage and anomaly detection for unusual access patterns.

mitigations.content: Implement resource quotas and cost controls with per-user spending limits, task complexity scoring, and automatic termination of runaway processes exceeding defined thresholds.

metadata.value: Prevents resource exhaustion attacks through proactive monitoring and enforcement. Includes predictive analysis to identify potentially expensive tasks before execution.

mitigations.content: Deploy certificate pinning and TLS inspection for GitHub API communications with fallback mechanisms and automated certificate validation to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks.

metadata.value: Enhances transport security beyond standard TLS with pinning validation and inspection capabilities. Includes monitoring for certificate anomalies and automatic failover.

mitigations.content: Implement AI model response filtering and sanitization to detect and remove potentially sensitive information before returning responses to users, with pattern matching for common data types.

metadata.value: Provides additional layer of protection against information leakage through AI model responses. Includes regex patterns for PII, credentials, and other sensitive data types.

mitigations.content: Deploy DynamoDB point-in-time recovery and cross-region backup with immutable audit logs stored in S3 with object lock to prevent tampering of critical task and event data.

metadata.value: Ensures audit trail integrity through immutable storage and enables recovery from data tampering incidents. Includes automated integrity checking and alerting for unauthorized changes.

mitigations.content: Implement agent memory validation and sanitization with content filtering to prevent injection of malicious learnings, including semantic analysis and anomaly detection for memory entries.

metadata.value: Protects cross-task learning system from poisoning attacks through validation and filtering of memory entries. Includes rollback capabilities for detected malicious content.

mitigations.content: Deploy API Gateway request/response logging with sensitive data masking and implement security information and event management (SIEM) integration for threat detection and response.

metadata.value: Provides comprehensive visibility into API usage patterns while protecting sensitive data. Includes automated threat detection rules and incident response playbooks.

mitigations.content: Implement least-privilege IAM policies with resource-level permissions and regular access reviews, using IAM conditions to restrict Lambda function access to only required AWS services and resources.

metadata.value: Reduces lateral movement risk through granular permission management and continuous access validation. Includes automated policy analysis and recommendations for further restriction.

mitigations.content: Deploy webhook request timestamping and nonce validation to prevent replay attacks, with configurable time windows and request deduplication using DynamoDB-based tracking.

metadata.value: Prevents webhook replay attacks through temporal validation and request uniqueness enforcement. Includes configurable tolerance windows for network latency considerations.

mitigations.content: Implement pull request validation and approval workflows with automated security scanning, requiring maintainer review for all agent-generated code changes before merge.

metadata.value: Ensures human oversight of all agent-generated code changes through mandatory review processes. Includes integration with security scanning tools and approval requirements.

mitigations.content: Enable DNS firewall enforcement mode with curated allowlists for approved domains and implement DNS over HTTPS (DoH) with trusted resolvers to prevent DNS manipulation attacks.

metadata.value: Transitions from observation to active protection mode with carefully managed domain allowlists. Includes monitoring for DNS anomalies and automated response capabilities.

mitigations.content: Deploy comprehensive log sanitization with automated PII detection and redaction, implementing structured logging with security-focused log analysis and retention policies.

metadata.value: Prevents sensitive data exposure through logs while maintaining operational visibility. Includes automated scanning for credentials, tokens, and PII with configurable redaction rules.

mitigations.content: Implement durable execution state validation and checkpointing with integrity verification to detect tampering attempts and prevent orchestration manipulation attacks.

metadata.value: Protects task orchestration from manipulation through cryptographic state validation and secure checkpointing. Includes automated recovery from detected tampering attempts.

mitigations.content: Deploy repository access validation with GitHub permissions checking before task execution, ensuring users can only target repositories they have legitimate access to through GitHub API verification.

metadata.value: Validates user permissions against target repositories before allowing task execution. Includes caching of permission checks and integration with GitHub's permission model.

threats.prerequisites: with access to compromised developer credentials
threats.threatAction: submit malicious coding tasks containing prompt injection attacks
threats.threatImpact: unauthorized code execution and potential data exfiltration from target repositories
threats.statement: A malicious actor with access to compromised developer credentials can submit malicious coding tasks containing prompt injection attacks, which leads to unauthorized code execution and potential data exfiltration from target repositories, resulting in reduced confidentiality of github repositories, source code, secrets

metadata.value: Despite Bedrock Guardrails, sophisticated prompt injection could bypass detection and manipulate agent behavior to execute unintended actions

threats.prerequisites: with network access to API Gateway
threats.threatAction: perform credential stuffing attacks against Cognito authentication
threats.threatImpact: unauthorized access to user accounts and ability to submit malicious tasks
threats.statement: An external attacker with network access to API Gateway can perform credential stuffing attacks against Cognito authentication, which leads to unauthorized access to user accounts and ability to submit malicious tasks, resulting in reduced confidentiality of user accounts, task data, authentication tokens

metadata.value: WAF rate limiting may not be sufficient against distributed credential stuffing attacks using valid username/password combinations from breaches

threats.prerequisites: with knowledge of webhook endpoint and valid HMAC signature
threats.threatAction: flood the system with high-volume task creation requests
threats.threatImpact: service denial and resource exhaustion affecting legitimate users
threats.statement: A malicious webhook caller with knowledge of webhook endpoint and valid HMAC signature can flood the system with high-volume task creation requests, which leads to service denial and resource exhaustion affecting legitimate users, resulting in reduced availability of task orchestrator, lambda functions, dynamodb tables

metadata.value: HMAC validation alone doesn't prevent abuse if webhook secrets are compromised or shared with untrusted systems

threats.prerequisites: with escape from MicroVM isolation
threats.threatAction: access other agent sessions or underlying infrastructure
threats.threatImpact: cross-tenant data exposure and potential infrastructure compromise
threats.statement: A compromised agent runtime with escape from MicroVM isolation can access other agent sessions or underlying infrastructure, which leads to cross-tenant data exposure and potential infrastructure compromise, resulting in reduced confidentiality of agent sessions, github tokens, task data

metadata.value: MicroVM isolation is critical security boundary - any escape could compromise entire system due to shared infrastructure

threats.prerequisites: with ability to manipulate GitHub repository content
threats.threatAction: inject malicious code or dependencies that get executed during agent analysis
threats.threatImpact: agent runtime compromise and potential lateral movement
threats.statement: A malicious actor with ability to manipulate GitHub repository content can inject malicious code or dependencies that get executed during agent analysis, which leads to agent runtime compromise and potential lateral movement, resulting in reduced integrity of agent runtime, github tokens, other repositories

metadata.value: Agent analyzes and executes code from repositories - malicious dependencies or build scripts could compromise the agent environment

threats.prerequisites: with access to AWS console or CDK deployment
threats.threatAction: modify infrastructure configuration to bypass security controls
threats.threatImpact: complete system compromise and data exfiltration
threats.statement: An insider threat with access to AWS console or CDK deployment can modify infrastructure configuration to bypass security controls, which leads to complete system compromise and data exfiltration, resulting in reduced confidentiality of entire system, user data, github tokens

metadata.value: CDK-based deployment means infrastructure changes can bypass application-level security controls

threats.prerequisites: with access to compromised GitHub tokens
threats.threatAction: access private repositories and exfiltrate sensitive source code
threats.threatImpact: intellectual property theft and exposure of proprietary algorithms
threats.statement: An attacker with access to compromised GitHub tokens can access private repositories and exfiltrate sensitive source code, which leads to intellectual property theft and exposure of proprietary algorithms, resulting in reduced confidentiality of private repositories, source code, intellectual property

metadata.value: GitHub tokens stored in Secrets Manager provide broad repository access - compromise could expose multiple private repositories

threats.prerequisites: with valid authentication
threats.threatAction: submit resource-intensive tasks to exhaust compute quotas
threats.threatImpact: service degradation and increased operational costs
threats.statement: A malicious user with valid authentication can submit resource-intensive tasks to exhaust compute quotas, which leads to service degradation and increased operational costs, resulting in reduced availability of agentcore runtime, lambda functions, bedrock quotas

metadata.value: User concurrency limits may not prevent resource exhaustion through complex or long-running tasks

threats.prerequisites: with ability to intercept network traffic
threats.threatAction: perform man-in-the-middle attacks on GitHub API communications
threats.threatImpact: data tampering and credential theft during repository operations
threats.statement: An attacker with ability to intercept network traffic can perform man-in-the-middle attacks on GitHub API communications, which leads to data tampering and credential theft during repository operations, resulting in reduced confidentiality of github communications, repository data, authentication tokens

metadata.value: Agent runtime communicates with GitHub API over internet - TLS validation and certificate pinning critical

threats.prerequisites: with access to task creation interface
threats.threatAction: inject malicious prompts designed to extract sensitive information from AI models
threats.threatImpact: exposure of training data or other users' information through model responses
threats.statement: A malicious actor with access to task creation interface can inject malicious prompts designed to extract sensitive information from AI models, which leads to exposure of training data or other users' information through model responses, resulting in reduced confidentiality of ai model responses, training data, user information

metadata.value: Bedrock Guardrails may not catch all sophisticated prompt injection techniques designed to extract model information

threats.prerequisites: with access to DynamoDB tables
threats.threatAction: modify task state or event records to hide malicious activity
threats.threatImpact: audit trail tampering and inability to detect security incidents
threats.statement: An attacker with access to DynamoDB tables can modify task state or event records to hide malicious activity, which leads to audit trail tampering and inability to detect security incidents, resulting in reduced integrity of task records, event logs, audit trails

metadata.value: DynamoDB access through IAM roles - compromise of Lambda execution role could allow data tampering

threats.prerequisites: with legitimate access to the system
threats.threatAction: poison the agent memory system with false or malicious learnings
threats.threatImpact: degraded agent performance and potential security bypass in future tasks
threats.statement: A malicious user with legitimate access to the system can poison the agent memory system with false or malicious learnings, which leads to degraded agent performance and potential security bypass in future tasks, resulting in reduced integrity of agent memory, cross-task learnings, future task execution

metadata.value: Agent memory stores cross-task learnings - malicious tasks could inject false information affecting future agent behavior

threats.prerequisites: with knowledge of system architecture
threats.threatAction: perform reconnaissance to identify vulnerable endpoints and services
threats.threatImpact: information gathering for targeted attacks against system components
threats.statement: An external attacker with knowledge of system architecture can perform reconnaissance to identify vulnerable endpoints and services, which leads to information gathering for targeted attacks against system components, resulting in reduced confidentiality of system architecture, endpoint information, service configuration

metadata.value: API Gateway endpoints and error responses could leak information about internal architecture and services

threats.prerequisites: with elevated IAM permissions
threats.threatAction: access resources beyond intended scope including other AWS services
threats.threatImpact: lateral movement and potential access to sensitive data in other services
threats.statement: A compromised lambda function with elevated IAM permissions can access resources beyond intended scope including other AWS services, which leads to lateral movement and potential access to sensitive data in other services, resulting in reduced confidentiality of aws services, sensitive data, iam roles

metadata.value: Lambda functions require various AWS service permissions - overly broad IAM policies could enable lateral movement

threats.prerequisites: with access to webhook secrets
threats.threatAction: replay valid webhook requests to trigger unauthorized task creation
threats.threatImpact: resource consumption and potential execution of unintended tasks
threats.statement: An attacker with access to webhook secrets can replay valid webhook requests to trigger unauthorized task creation, which leads to resource consumption and potential execution of unintended tasks, resulting in reduced integrity of task queue, compute resources, webhook system

metadata.value: HMAC validation doesn't prevent replay attacks - timestamp validation or nonce mechanisms needed

threats.prerequisites: with access to target repository
threats.threatAction: reject legitimate pull requests or accept malicious ones to disrupt development workflow
threats.threatImpact: development process disruption and potential introduction of vulnerabilities
threats.statement: A malicious repository maintainer with access to target repository can reject legitimate pull requests or accept malicious ones to disrupt development workflow, which leads to development process disruption and potential introduction of vulnerabilities, resulting in reduced integrity of development workflow, code quality, pull requests

metadata.value: System relies on human review of generated pull requests - malicious maintainers could subvert the process

threats.prerequisites: with ability to manipulate DNS responses
threats.threatAction: redirect agent network requests to malicious servers
threats.threatImpact: data exfiltration and potential agent compromise through malicious responses
threats.statement: An attacker with ability to manipulate DNS responses can redirect agent network requests to malicious servers, which leads to data exfiltration and potential agent compromise through malicious responses, resulting in reduced confidentiality of network communications, agent data, external api responses

metadata.value: DNS firewall in observation mode may not prevent malicious DNS responses - enforcement mode needed for protection

threats.prerequisites: with CloudWatch access
threats.threatAction: access sensitive information in logs including tokens or user data
threats.threatImpact: unauthorized access to confidential information through log analysis
threats.statement: A system administrator with CloudWatch access can access sensitive information in logs including tokens or user data, which leads to unauthorized access to confidential information through log analysis, resulting in reduced confidentiality of log data, user information, authentication tokens

metadata.value: Lambda functions and agent runtime may inadvertently log sensitive data - log sanitization critical

threats.prerequisites: with access to task orchestrator
threats.threatAction: manipulate durable execution state to cause task failures or infinite loops
threats.threatImpact: service disruption and resource exhaustion through orchestration manipulation
threats.statement: An attacker with access to task orchestrator can manipulate durable execution state to cause task failures or infinite loops, which leads to service disruption and resource exhaustion through orchestration manipulation, resulting in reduced availability of task orchestrator, durable execution, system resources

metadata.value: Durable execution with 9-hour timeout could be manipulated to create resource-intensive loops

threats.prerequisites: with CLI access
threats.threatAction: submit tasks targeting repositories they don't have legitimate access to
threats.threatImpact: unauthorized repository access and potential data exposure
threats.statement: A malicious developer with CLI access can submit tasks targeting repositories they don't have legitimate access to, which leads to unauthorized repository access and potential data exposure, resulting in reduced confidentiality of unauthorized repositories, repository data, access controls

metadata.value: System may not validate user permissions against target repositories before task execution
